<command-message>ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-scaffold-preflight</command-message>
<command-name>/ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-scaffold-preflight</command-name>
<command-args>## A2UI DAG EXECUTION SCAFFOLD PREFLIGHT Run only scaffold preflight now. Original user prompt: 为这个 HarmonyOS 工程实现一款平安扣的适老化App。为独居人群设计的安全工具。每 日签到，若连续多日未操作，系统会自动邮件通知紧急联系人，用极低成本提供安全感。除此之外还需要有AI伴聊、一键求助和AI伴聊功能，并完成构建和运行验证。 Tasks: 1. Check HarmonyOS scaffold markers in the current directory: build-profile.json5, oh-package.json5, entry/, entry/src/main/module.json5. 2. If markers are missing, create the correct scaffold by invoking the bundled create-harmony-project or create-atomic-service skill conservatively from the original prompt. 3. Read the standard key scaffold files that exist and produce a concise Scaffold Preflight Summary. Stop after the Scaffold Preflight Summary. Do not read .arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md or .arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json yet. Do not write .arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json, .arkpilot/plans/autopilot-impl.md, or feature implementation source files in this preflight pass. Wait for orchestration to resume this same execution lane after the semantic UI protocol is ready.</command-args>

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/autopilot-html-tmux-scaffold-preflight

<Purpose>
Use this skill at the start of the A2UI DAG Plan Agent session, before `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md` and `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json` exist.

It creates or verifies the HarmonyOS scaffold, reads the standard scaffold files, emits a concise `Scaffold Preflight Summary`, then stops. It does not write a handoff file and does not implement product features.

This skill owns project creation and scaffold verification for the A2UI execution flow. Later build skills should consume the verified scaffold instead of invoking project creation or running a duplicate scaffold preflight phase.
</Purpose>

<Inputs>
- User's original product prompt.
- Current project directory.
</Inputs>

<Project_Type_Selection>
Choose conservatively from the original prompt:

- Use `/ArkPilot:create-atomic-service` only when the prompt explicitly asks for `元服务`, `原子化服务`, `atomic service`, `Atomic Service`, `服务卡片`, or a clearly equivalent atomic-service target.
- Otherwise default to `/ArkPilot:create-harmony-project`.
- If a HarmonyOS scaffold already exists, do not recreate it. Infer the current scaffold type from actual project files when practical, and continue with pre-reading.
</Project_Type_Selection>

<Workflow>
1. Check scaffold markers in the current directory:
   - `build-profile.json5`
   - `oh-package.json5`
   - `entry/`
   - `entry/src/main/module.json5`
2. If any marker is missing, invoke the selected creation skill in the current directory:
   - Regular app: `/ArkPilot:create-harmony-project`
   - Atomic service / 元服务: `/ArkPilot:create-atomic-service`
3. After creation, verify the same markers again.
4. Pre-read the standard scaffold files that exist. Missing optional files should be reported in the summary, not treated as fatal:
   - `build-profile.json5`
   - `oh-package.json5`
   - `AppScope/app.json5`
   - `entry/src/main/module.json5`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/profile/main_pages.json`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/entryability/EntryAbility.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/color.json`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/string.json`
   - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/float.json`
5. If the scaffold contains the fixed bottom-tab shell files below, read them as scaffold evidence and mention the detected shell in the summary. Do not validate product behavior or rewrite the shell:
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/Theme.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/SvgIcon.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/common/TabTypes.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/components/BottomTabBar.ets`
   - `entry/src/main/ets/pages/Index.ets`
6. Output only a concise `Scaffold Preflight Summary` and stop.
</Workflow>

<Boundaries>
- Do not read `.arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md`.
- Do not read `.arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json`.
- Do not write `.arkpilot/plans/autopilot-impl.md`.
- Do not write any handoff or cache artifact for the preflight summary.
- Do not edit feature source files beyond scaffold creation by the existing project creation skill.
- Do not launch the app, start an emulator, run runtime UI verification, or take screenshots.
</Boundaries>

<Summary_Format>
End with this shape:

```text
Scaffold Preflight Summary
- Project type: app | atomic-service | existing-unknown
- Scaffold status: existing | created | blocked
- Marker evidence: ...
- Entry ability: ...
- Main page registry: ...
- Initial page: ...
- Fixed bottom-tab shell: detected | absent | partial
- Resource files present: ...
- Missing optional files: ...
- Notes for later impl plan: ...
```
</Summary_Format>

<Exit_Conditions>
- A valid scaffold exists, or a clear blocker is reported.
- Standard scaffold files have been read when present.
- The final response contains `Scaffold Preflight Summary`.
- No spec, UI tree, implementation plan, feature source edits, or handoff file were produced by this skill.
</Exit_Conditions>


ARGUMENTS: ## A2UI DAG EXECUTION SCAFFOLD PREFLIGHT Run only scaffold preflight now. Original user prompt: 为这个 HarmonyOS 工程实现一款平安扣的适老化App。为独居人群设计的安全工具。每 日签到，若连续多日未操作，系统会自动邮件通知紧急联系人，用极低成本提供安全感。除此之外还需要有AI伴聊、一键求助和AI伴聊功能，并完成构建和运行验证。 Tasks: 1. Check HarmonyOS scaffold markers in the current directory: build-profile.json5, oh-package.json5, entry/, entry/src/main/module.json5. 2. If markers are missing, create the correct scaffold by invoking the bundled create-harmony-project or create-atomic-service skill conservatively from the original prompt. 3. Read the standard key scaffold files that exist and produce a concise Scaffold Preflight Summary. Stop after the Scaffold Preflight Summary. Do not read .arkpilot/autopilot/spec.md or .arkpilot/designs/ui-tree.json yet. Do not write .arkpilot/designs/ui-behavior.json, .arkpilot/plans/autopilot-impl.md, or feature implementation source files in this preflight pass. Wait for orchestration to resume this same execution lane after the semantic UI protocol is ready.

addedLines: - ArkPilot:analyst: Requirements analyst for autopilot-standalone (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:architect: Technical architect and plan reviewer for autopilot-standalone (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:code-reviewer: Code reviewer for autopilot-standalone validation phase (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:critic: Plan critic for autopilot-standalone — final quality gate before execution (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- ArkPilot:executor: Agent from ArkPilot plugin (Tools: All tools)
- ArkPilot:explore: Codebase explorer for ArkPilot deep-interview brownfield mode (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash)

- ArkPilot:harmonyos-ui-validator: Validate HarmonyOS app UI against a test spec or checklist. Use proactively when the user asks to verify screens, flows, layout, controls, visibility, or interaction results in a HarmonyOS app. (Tools: All tools)
- ArkPilot:security-reviewer: Security reviewer for autopilot-standalone validation phase (READ-ONLY) (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit)
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

- ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-plan: Create a HarmonyOS implementation plan from a product idea, spec, mobile UI design, and HTML design artifacts
- ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-scaffold-preflight: Pre-create or verify a HarmonyOS app or atomic service scaffold from the user's initial prompt, read the fixed key project files, output a concise in-session summary, and stop before spec or UI tree planning.
- ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-team-build
- ArkPilot:autopilot-html-tmux-team-build-id: Implement HarmonyOS ArkTS from A2UI planning artifacts, semantic IDs, behavior contracts, and visual artifacts when available; delegate reusable ArkUI structure patterns to arkui-ui-patterns and stabilize the build
- ArkPilot:create-atomic-service
- ArkPilot:create-harmony-project: Create a HarmonyOS ArkTS project in the current workspace.
- ArkPilot:harmony-knowledge-search
- ArkPilot:harmonyos-core-flow-qa-fix
- ArkPilot:harmonyos-spec-to-api: Infer and verify the HarmonyOS ArkTS/ArkUI APIs needed for a product spec by researching a local docs repository and writing a split API reference output. Use this skill whenever the user wants to research HarmonyOS APIs for an app, map a spec to ArkTS APIs, or explore the local HarmonyOS docs directory structure. Also trigger when the user asks to "看看文档目录", "查一下鸿蒙API", or provides a spec.md and asks for API analysis.
- ArkPilot:ui-qa-svg-icon
- ArkPilot:ui-qa-tabbar
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

content.text: The directory only contains `.arkpilot/` — no scaffold markers present. I'll create the HarmonyOS project scaffold. The prompt is for a regular app (not 元服务), so I'll use the create-harmony-project skill.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/huaweiide/Desktop/fe/code/harmony-pilot/skills/create-harmony-project

# Create Harmony Project

Use this skill when the user asks for things like:

- "创建一个鸿蒙工程"
- "初始化一个 HarmonyOS 空项目"
- "给我一个 ArkTS 空白应用骨架"

This skill is for scaffolding a HarmonyOS project into the current workspace from a bundled template. It is not a general HarmonyOS coding guide.

## Workflow

1. **Do not check the workspace contents beforehand.**
2. **Do not ask the user for confirmation.**
3. Use the current workspace as the project directory instead of creating a new subdirectory.
4. Default the displayed app name to the current workspace directory name.
5. Only pass `--bundle-name`, `--vendor`, or other metadata overrides when the user explicitly asks for them. Do not ask proactively.
6. Do not handwrite the scaffold from scratch when the bundled template is sufficient.
7. Run the scaffold script immediately and return its output:

```bash
python3 SKILL_DIRECTORY/scripts/create_project.py "$PWD"
```

Optional overrides:

```bash
python3 SKILL_DIRECTORY/scripts/create_project.py "$PWD" \
  --project-name WorkspaceName \
  --bundle-name com.example.newapp \
  --vendor example
```

## What the script does

- copies the contents of `assets/empty-project/` into the requested workspace directory
- skips local machine files such as `.DS_Store`, `.idea`, and `local.properties`
- allows the destination directory to already exist, but stops on file collisions instead of overwriting existing project files
- updates:
  - `AppScope/app.json5`
  - `AppScope/resources/base/element/string.json`
  - `entry/src/main/resources/base/element/string.json`

## Guardrails

- Treat `assets/empty-project/` as the source of truth for the scaffold.
- Prefer using the current workspace name as the app name when the user does not provide one.
- Unless the user explicitly specifies metadata such as `bundleName` or `vendor`, keep the template defaults.
- If the current workspace already contains colliding project files, stop with a clear error instead of overwriting them.

content.thinking:
The template scaffold ships a fixed bottom-tab shell (Theme.ets, SvgIcon.ets, TabTypes.ets, BottomTabBar.ets) but Index.ets imports pages that don't exist yet (HomePage, ExplorePage, etc.) — those are template placeholders. Let me note the shell is detected but the referenced pages (HomePage, ExplorePage, OrdersPage, RewardsPage, DetailPage, EditorPage, ProfilePage) are absent.

Let me produce the summary.

content.text:
All markers verified. Scaffold preflight complete.

```text
Scaffold Preflight Summary
- Project type: app
- Scaffold status: created
- Marker evidence: build-profile.json5 ✓, oh-package.json5 ✓, entry/ ✓, entry/src/main/module.json5 ✓ (all absent before creation, present after running create-harmony-project into $PWD)
- Entry ability: entry/src/main/ets/entryability/EntryAbility.ets — standard UIAbility, loads 'pages/Index'; mainElement = EntryAbility, type=entry, phone, launcher skills present
- Main page registry: entry/src/main/resources/base/profile/main_pages.json → ["pages/Index"]
- Initial page: pages/Index
- Fixed bottom-tab shell: detected — Theme.ets, SvgIcon.ets, TabTypes.ets, BottomTabBar.ets all present
- Resource files present: color.json (start_window_background), string.json (module_desc, EntryAbility_desc, EntryAbility_label="test1"), float.json (page_text_font_size); AppScope/app.json5 present (bundleName com.example.myapplication, label placeholder)
- Missing optional files: none of the standard key files are missing. NOTE: Index.ets references placeholder pages that do NOT exist in the template — HomePage, ExplorePage, OrdersPage, RewardsPage, DetailPage, EditorPage, ProfilePage. The build will fail until these page modules are either implemented or the Index.ets surface routing is replaced.
- Notes for later impl plan:
  • Template uses a fixed BottomTabBar + inline tab surface / full-page overlay navigation model (Stack{ Column{CurrentSurface}; BottomTabBar }). Keep BottomTabBar inside that Stack.
  • App is a 4-tab shell (tab-home/explore/orders/rewards) by default — will need to be repurposed for 平安扣 features: daily check-in (签到), multi-day-inactivity emergency-contact email alert, AI 伴聊 (chat), one-tap SOS (一键求助). Tab labels/icons/pages and AppSurface union must be reworked to match the平安扣 IA rather than the commerce-style placeholders.
  • bundleName/icon/label (com.example.myapplication, $string:app_name from AppScope) should be updated to a 平安扣 identity; string.json already localized EntryAbility_label to "test1".
  • Entry point and main_pages.json start at pages/Index; new pages (CheckIn, SOS, Chat, Profile/Contacts) must be registered here when added.
  • Build will not compile in current template state because imported placeholder pages are undefined — first impl step must replace Index.ets routing and create the real page modules.
```
